vulnerabilities.title: closeMarket can finalize with bad debt when repayment transfers credit less than requested
vulnerabilities.summary:
When `currentlyHeld < totalDebts`, `closeMarket` performs `_repay(state, remainingDebt, 0x04)` and then unconditionally increments `currentlyHeld` by `remainingDebt`. If the underlying transfer credits less than requested, market solvency is overestimated and `isClosed` is still set. The function then computes `availableLiquidity` from the inflated local variable and can complete closure while real `totalAssets()` is still below liabilities.

Post-close, lenders can queue withdrawals; because th

description.desc: `closeMarket` assumes `_repay(remainingDebt)` increases assets by exactly `remainingDebt` and updates a local `currentlyHeld` variable with `currentlyHeld += remainingDebt` instead of re-reading `totalAssets()`.

vulnerabilities.impact: Lenders can suffer permanent principal shortfall after a seemingly successful market closure; borrower liabilities are prematurely finalized despite unresolved debt.
vulnerabilities.proof_of_concept: 1. Borrower uses an underlying token whose `transferFrom` credits the market less than the requested amount. 2. Lenders deposit and borrower borrows. 3. Borrower calls `closeMarket`; `_repay` requests `remainingDebt` but market receives less. 4. `closeMarket` still does `currentlyHeld += remainingDebt` and closes the market. 5. Lenders start withdrawing; only part of their batch is paid. 6. Next block, the unpaid portion is pushed to `unpaidBatches`. 7. Because the market is closed, unpaid batches cannot be processed through normal repayment/processing flow, so lender funds remain locked/lost.
vulnerabilities.remediation: In `closeMarket`, recompute `currentlyHeld` from `totalAssets()` after each transfer rather than trusting nominal transfer amounts. Add an explicit solvency check before setting `isClosed` (e.g., require `totalAssets() >= state.totalDebts()`). Also provide a valid post-close path to process unpaid batches or prevent closure if any underfunding can later create unpaid batches.
